title: [InstCombine] Do not perform binop-of-shuffle when mask is poison
summary: The generalization expands from a strict zeroinitializer shuffle mask to allow any splat mask where the first element is not poison, enabling detection of partial splats. The original pattern matched only `zeroinitializer` masks, but the patch replaces `m_ZeroMask()` with `m_Mask(Mask)` and adds a check for `Mask[0] != PoisonMaskElem`. This allows the optimization to recognize shufflevectors that broadcast element 0 (or any non-poison element) across the vector, even if other mask indices are poison. The key insight is that as long as the demanded element corresponds to a defined splat source, the binop can be reused. Developers can apply this by relaxing exact mask matching to check only relevant indices, using `PoisonMaskElem` to safely ignore undefined positions while ensuring demanded elements are defined.
